content.claim: Roma students face severe early school-leaving rates: in 2023, 58.7% of Roma students (18–24-year-olds) were school dropouts compared with 9.3% among non-Roma; disadvantaged students and rural residents are overrepresented among early leavers; only 12.1% of disadvantaged students in Hungary reach good proficiency in basic skills (reading, mathematics, science) versus 16.3% EU average.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: System-wide outcome gaps documented but not proof that 6/8-year gimnázium specifically (versus broader 3-track secondary structure) is primary cause
Early leaving concentrates in vocational tracks rather than gimnázium non-admission specifically
Data reflect cumulative sorting effects; cannot isolate causal contribution of age-10/12 selection from other mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
